title: microlaena stipoides subsp. stipoides
intro: Microlaena stipoides subsp. stipoides, also known as the panicled microlaena, is a perennial grass native to Australia and New Zealand. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly as an anti-inflammatory and antiseptic agent. The plant has been traditionally used to treat a variety of health issues, including wound healing, skin conditions, and digestive problems, with its health benefits attributed to its ability to soothe and calm the body. Herbal preparations of microlaena stipoides subsp. stipoides include teas, infusions, and salves, which can be made by infusing the leaves and roots in hot water or oil to create a medicinal solution.
